rulings.Case Title: SHENG ZHONG, ET AL VS. BEN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 20, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT BEN WONG'S Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint is denied. Defendant presents an insufficient explanation for the delay in bringing this motion and does not address why he learned about the non payment of rent this late in the process. Defendant, as the landlord, was presumably in possession of facts regarding nonpayment for over a year. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRET LUEDER VS. BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 20, 2010, line 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, DMV DIRECTOR GEORGE VALVERDE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Plaintiff has not complied with the claims requirement of the Government Claims Act, and there is no common-law liability against DMV. No viable federal constitutional claim is alleged. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BRET LUEDER VS. BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 20, 2010, line 11. DEFENDANT BAY AREA TOLL AUTHORITY, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR OF THE METROPOLITAN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Plaintiff has not complied with the claims requirement of the Government Claims Act, and there is no common-law liability against BATA. Plaintiff's claim is now time-barred. No viable claim is alleged. =(302/CWW)
